title: Cast before calling __Vdpiimwrap_ function may yield wrong address in VerilatedDpiOpenVar
body:
### Context:

I am attempting to access 2-state bit data of top level signals in a Verilated model by address.
I am assuming the address of these global signals is constant once they are allocated.
The signals are declared as system verilog type "bit".

Versions:
% >g++ --version
g++ (Ubuntu 11.4.0-1ubuntu1~22.04.3) 11.4.0
% >verilator --version
Verilator 4.038 2020-07-11 rev v4.036-114-g0cd4a57ad

or 

% >verilator --version
Verilator 5.047 devel rev v5.046-95-gc0d018091

### The Problem:

The addresses of bit arrays passed as OpenArrays appeared to be wrong for bit arrays containing
from 1 to 16 bits. Bit arrays 17 bits or larger pass correct addresses.

An example of the form of DPI import in system verilog I used is:

```
    import "DPI-C" context function void dpi_testOpenArrayPtr( //
        input bit a[],  //
        input bit b[],  //
        input bit c[],  //
        input bit d[],  //
        input bit e[],  //
        input bit f[]); //

    bit single_bit /* verilator public_flat */;
    bit [7:0] eight_bit /* verilator public_flat */;
    bit [15:0] sixteen_bit /* verilator public_flat */;
    bit [16:0] seventeen_bit /* verilator public_flat */;
    bit [31:0] thirtytwo_bit /* verilator public_flat */;
    bit [63:0] sixtyfour_bit /* verilator public_flat */;

    initial begin
        dpi_testOpenArrayPtr(single_bit, eight_bit, sixteen_bit, seventeen_bit, thirtytwo_bit, sixtyfour_bit);
        end

```
The C++ code of the same example is:

```
    uint8_t *tbit, *tint8;
    uint16_t *tint16;
    uint32_t *tint17,*tint32;
    uint64_t *tint64;

    extern "C" void dpi_testOpenArrayPtr(    //
        const svOpenArrayHandle a_bit,   //
        const svOpenArrayHandle a_int8,  //
        const svOpenArrayHandle a_int16, //
        const svOpenArrayHandle a_int17, //
        const svOpenArrayHandle a_int32, //
        const svOpenArrayHandle a_int64  //
    ) {
        tbit   = static_cast<uint8_t *>(svGetArrayPtr(a_bit));
        tint8  = static_cast<uint8_t *>(svGetArrayPtr(a_int8));
        tint16 = static_cast<uint16_t *>(svGetArrayPtr(a_int16));
        tint32 = static_cast<uint32_t *>(svGetArrayPtr(a_int17));
        tint32 = static_cast<uint32_t *>(svGetArrayPtr(a_int32));
        tint64 = static_cast<uint64_t *>(svGetArrayPtr(a_int64));
    }

```
Using both the Ubuntu 22.04 package and the master branch from building
```
    git clone https://github.com/verilator/verilator
```
I get in the call of the generated __Vdpiimwrap_ function the following:

```
    vlTOPp->__Vdpiimwrap_dpiTest__DOT__dpi_testOpenArrayPtr__Vdpioc2_TOP(
            (&(vlSymsp->__Vscope_dpiTest)),
            "dpiTest.v", 0x1dU,
            (IData)(vlTOPp->dpiTest__DOT__single_bit),
            vlTOPp->dpiTest__DOT__eight_bit,
            (IData)(vlTOPp->dpiTest__DOT__sixteen_bit),
            vlTOPp->dpiTest__DOT__seventeen_bit,
            vlTOPp->dpiTest__DOT__thirtytwo_bit,
            vlTOPp->dpiTest__DOT__sixtyfour_bit);

```
Note that the 8, 17, 32, and 64 bit arrays have no cast on their call arguments, but
the 1 and 16 bit are cast to IData.

I observed IN MY LARGE APPLICATION that the call arguments without a cast yield the
correct addresses in the verilatedDpiOpenVar (called svOpenArrayHandle), and those with
the cast do not. IN MY LARGE APPLICATION if I edit the generated __Vdpiimwrap_ function
call to eliminate the casts and rebuild the model, I get the correct addresses and
the references to inside the model using those addresses appear to work properly.

As reality would have it, the small test case I constructed only demonstrates the same
presence of the cast operators in the generated call arguments to the __Vdpiimwrap_
function, but in the small test case the addresses seem to be correct both with and without
the cast. So my attempt to supply a test case failed in demonstrating a wrong result even
though it succceeded in showing the same generated cast arguments.

My work around is to use an editor like sed to find and remove the cast operators from the
generated code and get on with what I need to get done. I had hoped to be able to work out
a fix to the verilator code to accomplish this, but the generation of the __Vdpiimwrap_ call
appears to have been merged into a visit function, requiring more hours determining internal
context than I can spare.
